title: plexipus insularis
side_effects: Plexipus insularis can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach pain, which may be caused by its high concentration of toxic alkaloids. Prolonged exposure to the plant's extract may lead to respiratory problems, including shortness of breath and bronchitis. In severe cases, improper use of plexipus insularis can cause neurological issues, including tremors, seizures, and even temporary paralysis. If not handled correctly, the plant's toxic compounds can also cause allergic reactions, such as skin irritation and anaphylaxis.
